qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via remote file import in participatory processes
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes UUID collisions in private data exports causing data leaks between users. This finding describes SSRF via participatory process imports with remote URLs. These are completely different vulnerabilities in different components with different root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The qualifying vulnerability does not match the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory (GHSA-3cx6-j9j4-54mp) describes a UUID collision vulnerability in Decidim's private data export feature that can cause data leaks between users. The qualifying finding describes SSRF via participatory process imports with remote URLs, which is unrelated to UUID handling or private exports. The finding does not identify the same vulnerable function, missing guard, or root-cause mechanism as the advisory.
evidence: Advisory root cause: UUID collision in private data exports when UUID.to_i conversion produces identical values
Advisory vulnerable component: Decidim private exports and download_your_data functionality
Advisory impact: Data leak between users when UUID collisions occur
Qualifying finding (ssrf-file-import-vulnerability): SSRF in participatory process import via remote URLs - unrelated to UUID handling or private exports
No overlap in vulnerable code locations, functions, or root-cause mechanisms
